entities.name: GHANA
entities.description: The country where the agricultural adaptation policy regime is analyzed

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE ADAPTATION POLICIES
entities.description: Policies designed to address the impacts of climate change on agriculture

entities.name: MULTILEVEL STAKEHOLDER INFLUENCE MAPPING
entities.description: A power-mapping technique used to provide quantitative influence scores and visual maps for actor groups

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL ADAPTATION POLICY REGIME
entities.description: The system of policies and actors involved in agricultural adaptation in Ghana

entities.name: STAKEHOLDER PARTICIPATION
entities.description: The involvement of various actor groups in the policy process

entities.name: MULTILEVEL INSTITUTIONAL DESIGN
entities.description: The structure of institutions across national, regional, and local levels

entities.name: POWER DISTRIBUTION
entities.description: The way power and influence are allocated among actors in the adaptation regime

entities.name: TECHNICAL GOVERNMENT AGENCIES
entities.description: Government bodies providing technical expertise, identified as part of the dominant power group

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL AGENCIES
entities.description: Global organizations identified as part of the dominant power group in the regime

entities.name: POLITICAL GROUPS
entities.description: Actors involved in political processes, currently wielding less power in the regime

entities.name: PRIVATE SECTOR
entities.description: Commercial entities involved in the agricultural sector with limited power in the regime

entities.name: CIVIL SOCIETY
entities.description: Non-governmental organizations and community groups with limited power in the regime

entities.name: UNIVERSITIES
entities.description: Academic institutions with limited power in the regime

entities.name: FARMERS
entities.description: Agricultural producers who are highly influential locally but less so nationally

entities.name: TRADITIONAL AUTHORITIES
entities.description: Local leaders with high influence at the local level but low influence at the national level

entities.name: DISTRICT ASSEMBLY
entities.description: Local government bodies with high influence at the local level but low influence at the national level

entities.name: POWER PLURALISM
entities.description: The distribution of power across a diverse range of actor groups and levels

entities.name: CROSS-LEVEL COOPERATION
entities.description: Collaboration between formal and informal institutions across different administrative levels
